free.label: Free project
free.description: A single canvas with the full palette. Best for fast prototyping.

httpClient.name: HTTP Client
httpClient.description: Sends HTTP requests to a server

clientGroup.name: Client Group
clientGroup.description: Simulates multiple virtual clients for stress testing

cdn.name: CDN
cdn.description: Edge cache for static and dynamic content

apiService.name: API Service
apiService.description: REST/gRPC API service hosted in a server or container

identityProvider.name: Identity Provider
identityProvider.description: Centralized identity provider (Keycloak, Auth0, Cognito, Okta)

properties.title: Properties
properties.noSelection: Select a component to view its properties
properties.name: Name

help.interval: Delay between two requests in loop mode. 1000 ms = 1 RPS ; 10 ms = 100 RPS. For stress-testing, see the ClientGroup component instead.
help.count: Total number of requests to emit. 0 or empty = unlimited (loops continuously until manual stop).

help.port: TCP listen port. Range 1–65535. Conventions : 80 (HTTP), 443 (HTTPS), 8080/8443 (proxy), >1024 for non-privileged services.
help.delay: Average internal server latency (application processing). Typical range : 10–200 ms. Simple CRUD API ≈ 20 ms ; API with DB joins ≈ 100–500 ms.
help.errorRate: Base error rate injected into the simulation (excluding resource-saturation errors). Typical range : 0–5 %. Above 1 %, consider an upstream circuit breaker.

help.readLatency: Average time to execute a SELECT. PostgreSQL OLTP ≈ 1–10 ms ; complex joins 50–200 ms. Consider a cache for <1 ms.
help.writeLatency: Average time to execute an INSERT/UPDATE. PostgreSQL ≈ 5–20 ms (synchronous commit). Higher with multiple indexes or triggers.
help.maxQps: Maximum queries-per-second capacity before saturation. Indicative : PostgreSQL m5.large ≈ 5,000 ; Aurora write-leader ≈ 50,000. Beyond, quadratic degradation.
help.errorRate: Base error rate (deadlock, sporadic timeout). Typical range : 0–0.5 %. Above → audit long queries.
help.maxConnections: Pool upper bound. PostgreSQL : ~100 per instance (memory-bound). Beyond : use pgBouncer.

help.initialHitRatio: Ratio of requests served from cache (vs miss going to source). Typical range : 60–95 %. <50 % → undersized cache or TTL too short.
help.defaultTTL: Default lifetime of an entry. Sessions : 1800 s ; CDN pages : 86400 s ; reference lookups : 3600–86400 s.
help.maxMemory: Memory allocated to cache. Beyond, entries are evicted per policy. Typical Redis cache-aside : 1–32 GB per instance.
help.getLatency: GET latency. Local Redis : 0.1–1 ms ; Memcached : 0.2 ms ; regional cache : 1–5 ms.

apiGateway.autoTokenDescription: Controls the auto-generated token when no IdP is connected

simulation.noComponents: Add components to start the simulation

ecommerce.name: E-Commerce Microservices
ecommerce.description: E-commerce platform with network zones, microservices and async communication

taxSystem.name: Provincial Tax System
taxSystem.description: Tax system: declarations, tax calculation, payments, nightly batch processing (35 nodes)

medicalCentral.name: Medical Data Centralization
medicalCentral.description: Health records with 5 medical subgroups, read/write separation, event pipeline (42 nodes)

bankingMultipole.name: Multi-Pole Banking Group
bankingMultipole.description: Banking group with 3 poles, 3 provinces, internal apps, cross-province replication (48 nodes)

bankingMultipoleC4.name: Multi-Pole Banking (C4)
bankingMultipoleC4.description: C4 multi-systems: 3 poles (Banking/Insurance/Accounting) with parentSystemId, 4 traffic-generating actors, L1→L2→L3 refinement via parentEdgeId. Cascade simulation demo.

bankingOnline.name: Online Banking (demo)
bankingOnline.description: Focused banking journey: clients → WAF/CDN/API GW → Auth + 2 services → DB + Redis + Kafka + Circuit Breaker to External Payment (14 nodes)

cqrs.name: Event-Driven CQRS
cqrs.description: Command/query separation via event bus: Command Service writes the write DB and emits events; a projection worker materializes a read view consumed by the Query Service

serverlessApi.name: Serverless API
serverlessApi.description: API Gateway in front of a cloud function (FaaS) that writes to object storage and a metadata DB (cold start simulated)

monolithDecomposition.name: Monolith Decomposition
monolithDecomposition.description: Strangler-fig pattern: legacy monolith and extracted microservice behind the same API Gateway, gradually routing traffic to the new service

bff.name: Backend-for-Frontend
bff.description: A mobile BFF and a web BFF in front of a shared service layer (user, catalog, order) to optimize payloads and per-channel aggregation

latency.title: Latency Calculator
latency.noPath: No path found between these nodes

pwa.offline: You are offline. The app works in local mode.
pwa.installMessage: Install ARCH.SIM on your device for quick offline access.

waterfall.noTraces: No traces captured. Start the simulation.

rules.httpClientNoOutgoing: HTTP client "{label}" has no outgoing connection
rules.clientGroupNoOutgoing: Client group "{label}" has no outgoing connection
rules.loadBalancerNoBackends: Load balancer "{label}" has no connected backends
rules.edgeDanglingTarget: A connection points to a non-existent node (ID: {nodeId})
rules.edgeDanglingSource: A connection comes from a non-existent node (ID: {nodeId})
rules.edgeToHostNoPort: Connection to server "{label}" has no target port configured
rules.edgeToFirewallNoPort: Connection to firewall "{label}" has no target port
rules.firewallBlocksAll: Firewall "{label}" blocks all traffic (deny with no allowed ports)
rules.portMappingDangling: Port mapping on "{label}" references a non-existent container
rules.portMismatch: Port {port} doesn't match any port mapping on "{label}"
rules.apiGatewayDanglingRoute: Route rule "{pattern}" on "{label}" targets unconnected service "{service}"
rules.apiGatewayRulesNoEdges: API Gateway "{label}" has route rules but no outgoing connections
rules.clientGroupOverloads: Group "{clientLabel}" ({clients} clients) targets a server limited to {maxConc} connections
rules.httpServerNoResources: HTTP server "{label}" has no resource configuration
rules.circuitBreakerNoIncoming: Circuit breaker "{label}" has no incoming connections (useless)
rules.circuitBreakerNoOutgoing: Circuit breaker "{label}" has no outgoing connections (no service to protect)
rules.cacheNoFallback: Cache "{label}" has no outgoing connection (no DB fallback on cache miss)
rules.cdnNoOrigin: CDN "{label}" has no origin server connected (hit ratio: {hitRatio}%, misses will fail)
rules.loadBalancerSingleBackend: Load balancer "{label}" has only 1 backend (nothing to balance)
rules.messageQueueNoConsumers: Queue "{label}" has no connected consumers (messages never processed)
rules.hostServerMappingsNoIncoming: Server "{label}" has port mappings but no incoming traffic
rules.apiGatewayDuplicatePriority: API Gateway "{label}" has {count} rules with same priority {priority} (non-deterministic routing)
rules.apiServiceNoName: Service "{label}" has no service name (required for API Gateway routing)
rules.serverlessTimeoutLessThanColdStart: Timeout ({timeout}ms) of "{label}" is less than cold start ({coldStart}ms)
rules.serverlessZeroConcurrency: "{label}" has concurrency limit of 0 (no requests can be processed)
rules.backgroundJobCronNoSchedule: Cron job "{label}" has no schedule defined
rules.backgroundJobBatchNoSize: Batch job "{label}" has no batch size defined
rules.httpClientZeroInterval: HTTP client "{label}" is in loop mode with 0ms interval (maximum spam)
rules.highErrorRate: Server "{label}" has an error rate of {rate}% (very high)
rules.lbWeightedNoWeights: Load balancer "{label}" uses weighted algorithm (default weight = 1 for all)
rules.circuitBreakerTightTimeout: Circuit breaker "{label}" has very short timeout ({timeout}ms)
rules.containerNoParent: Container "{label}" is not hosted in a server
rules.containerWrongParent: Container "{label}" is inside "{parentLabel}" instead of a host-server
rules.portMappingNotChild: Port mapping {port} on "{label}" references a container that is not a child of this server

auth001.title: Public endpoints must require authentication
auth001.description: An endpoint reachable from a client without authentication exposes all data without access control.
auth001.remediation: Configure authType=jwt (or oauth2) on the public API Gateway / HTTP Server / API Service.

auth002.title: MFA enabled on Identity Providers
auth002.description: An IdP without MFA is vulnerable to credential stuffing and password spraying.
auth002.remediation: Enable mfaEnabled: true on the Identity Provider component.

net001.title: WAF in front of public APIs
net001.description: An endpoint reachable from a client without an upstream WAF is vulnerable to OWASP Top 10 attacks (injection, XSS, etc.).
net001.remediation: Insert a WAF component on the path client → endpoint.

net002.title: Databases must never be in DMZ zone
net002.description: A database placed in DMZ is potentially exposed to external traffic — defense in depth violation.
net002.remediation: Move the database to a Backend or Data zone.

net003.title: Sensitive components must be inside a network zone
net003.description: Database, Cache and Identity Provider must be contained inside a network zone (segmentation).
net003.remediation: Drag the component inside a zone (Backend, Data, or private Backend).

rate001.title: Rate limiting required on public gateways
rate001.description: A public API Gateway without rate limiting is exposed to DoS, brute force, and scraping.
rate001.remediation: Enable rateLimiting.enabled=true on the API Gateway and configure requestsPerSecond.

res001.title: Circuit breaker required on calls to external systems
res001.description: An internal service calling an external system without a circuit breaker can cascade its failure.
res001.remediation: Insert a Circuit Breaker between the internal service and the external call.

res002.title: Connection pool configured on databases
res002.description: A DB without a configured pool opens connections on demand, risking resource exhaustion.

dlq001.title: Dead Letter Queue enabled on Message Queues
dlq001.description: An MQ without a DLQ loses messages that fail beyond the max retry count.
dlq001.remediation: Enable deadLetterEnabled: true on the Message Queue.

dos001.title: Reasonable auth failure rate
dos001.description: An authFailureRate > 5% is abnormally high and may indicate an attack or misconfiguration.
dos001.remediation: Lower authFailureRate to <5% in production. Audit the cause if real.

welcome.title: Welcome to Architecture Simulator
welcome.description: This interactive tutorial will guide you step by step to create your first distributed architecture and run a simulation. Follow each step to discover the main features.

projectSelector.title: Project Selector
projectSelector.description: Manage your projects from this menu. Each project can contain multiple architecture diagrams. Click to create, rename, duplicate, or delete a project.

diagramTabs.title: Diagram Tabs
diagramTabs.description: Each project can contain multiple diagrams. Use these tabs to navigate between your diagrams, create new ones (+), or manage them (rename, duplicate, delete) via the context menu.

componentsPanel.title: Components Panel
componentsPanel.description: This panel (RACK) contains 20 components organized in 7 categories: Simulation, Infrastructure, Data, Resilience, Compute, Cloud and Zones. Drag and drop a component onto the canvas to add it to your architecture.

headerTools.title: Toolbar
headerTools.description:
**TPL** — load a predefined architecture
**YAML** — define architecture in YAML code
**VALID** — validate architecture consistency
**Trash** — delete all components from the canvas
**DOCS** — access the documentation

undoRedoSnapshots.title: Undo / Redo & Snapshots
undoRedoSnapshots.description:
**Undo** (Ctrl+Z) — undo the last change
**Redo** (Ctrl+Y) — redo the undone change
**SNAP** — save a snapshot of the current architecture
**History** (⏱) — restore or delete a previous snapshot

flowCanvasIntro.title: Workspace Canvas
flowCanvasIntro.description: This is where you build your architecture. Drag components from the RACK, connect them together, and organize your diagram. You can zoom, pan the view, and select components to configure them.

dragHttpClient.title: Add an HTTP Client
dragHttpClient.description: Drag the "HTTP Client" component from the left panel and drop it onto the canvas.

dragHttpServer.title: Add an HTTP Server
dragHttpServer.description: Now drag the "HTTP Server" component and drop it onto the canvas, to the right of the client.

connectNodes.title: Connect the Components
connectNodes.description: Click and hold the connection point (●) on the right side of the HTTP Client, then drag to the input point (●) on the left side of the HTTP Server to create a connection.

architectureReady.title: Architecture Ready!
architectureReady.description: Well done! Your first architecture is created. Tip: click on a component to edit its properties in the right panel (HTTP method, port, response delay, etc.).

selectHttpClient.title: Select the HTTP Client
selectHttpClient.description: Click on the "HTTP Client" component on the canvas to select it and open the properties panel.

propertiesPanelIntro.title: Properties Panel
propertiesPanelIntro.description: This panel shows the properties of the selected component. You can modify the HTTP method, path, request mode, interval, and many other parameters.

setLoopMode.title: Enable Continuous Loop
setLoopMode.description: Change the request mode from "Single Request" to "Continuous Loop". This will allow the client to send requests continuously during the simulation.

switchSimMode.title: Switch to Simulation Mode
switchSimMode.description: Click "SIM" to switch to simulation mode. In simulation mode, the architecture is locked and you can run load tests.

simControlsIntro.title: Simulation Controls
simControlsIntro.description:
**START** — launch the simulation
**PAUSE** — pause it
**STOP** — stop and generate the report
**RST** — reset
**DUR** — configure the maximum duration

startSimulation.title: Start the Simulation
startSimulation.description: Click START to launch the simulation. The HTTP client will start sending requests to the server.

observeParticles.title: Simulation Running
observeParticles.description: Watch the animated particles flowing between components. They represent requests in transit. Nodes change color based on their state (processing, success, error).

pauseSimulation.title: Pause the Simulation
pauseSimulation.description: Click PAUSE to suspend the simulation. This will let you explore the collected metrics at your own pace.

analyzeButton.title: Analyze Button
analyzeButton.description: This button appears when the simulation is paused or finished. Click it to open the full analysis view: health score, performance, resources, bottlenecks, traces, and recommendations.

metricsTab.title: Metrics Tab
metricsTab.description: The Metrics tab displays real-time performance metrics: latency (min/avg/max), success rate, requests per second (RPS), and server resource utilization with sparklines.

outputTab.title: Output Tab
outputTab.description: The Output tab displays a real-time request log: each request sent and its response with status code, latency, and the path taken.

validationTab.title: Validation Tab
validationTab.description: The Valid tab shows architecture validation results: blocking errors (disconnected components, invalid configurations) and warnings.

tracesTab.title: Traces Tab
tracesTab.description: The Traces tab displays a waterfall view of request chains. You can visualize the complete path of a request through components with detailed timings.

stopSimulation.title: Stop the Simulation
stopSimulation.description: Click STOP to stop the simulation. A detailed report will be automatically generated with all collected metrics.

viewReport.title: Simulation Report
viewReport.description: This report summarizes your architecture's performance: total duration, success rate, average latency, active client count, and server resource saturation. Use it to identify bottlenecks.

installPwa.title: Install the App
installPwa.description: ARCH.SIM is a Progressive Web App: you can install it on your device for quick offline access. If an install banner appears at the bottom of the screen, click "Install" to add the app to your desktop.

projects.title: Projects
projects.deleteConfirm: Delete this project and all its diagrams?

custom.title: Custom rules
custom.description: YAML DSL for project-specific architecture rules. The `project-custom` pack is recompiled on every apply.

blockingDialog.title: Simulation blocked
blockingDialog.subtitle: {count} critical error(s) prevent the simulation from starting.
blockingDialog.fixHint: Fix the issues above, then re-run the simulation.

dialog.title: Suppress rule {ruleId}
dialog.reasonPlaceholder: E.g. pre-prod debug DB, intentional for this sprint…

db-cannot-initiate.message: A database cannot initiate outbound connections.

cache-cannot-initiate.message: A cache cannot initiate a connection.

cdn-cannot-target-client.message: A CDN cannot target a client: the client is the one calling the CDN.

cache-cannot-target-client.message: A cache cannot directly target a client.

db-cannot-target-client.message: A database cannot directly target a client.

client-cannot-target-client.message: Two clients cannot be directly connected to each other.

firewall-cannot-initiate.message: A firewall or WAF cannot initiate traffic — it only filters.

dns-cannot-be-target-of-data.message: A DNS cannot be the target of application traffic (resolution only).

identity-provider-cannot-initiate.message: An identity provider cannot initiate a request to an application service.

queue-direct-to-queue.message: A queue cannot be directly connected to another queue (a consumer is required between them).

client-direct-to-db.message: Client connected directly to a database — no service in front. Security risk.

client-direct-to-cache.message: Client connected directly to a cache — unusual access path.

lb-targets-lb.message: A load balancer targets another load balancer — LB chain rare in production.

serverless-targets-client.message: A serverless function targets a client — unusual push pattern, verify intent.

orphan-circuit-breaker.message: Circuit breaker traversed by a single edge with no fan-out: it serves no purpose in this configuration.

orphan-node.title: Unconnected node
orphan-node.message: This node has no incoming or outgoing edges.
orphan-node.description: An unconnected component does not participate in any simulation flow.
orphan-node.suggestion: Connect this node to at least one other component, or delete it.

cycle-detected.title: Cycle in dependency graph
cycle-detected.message: A cycle has been detected in the dependency graph.
cycle-detected.description: Cycles can cause recursive calls without backpressure and confuse critical-path analysis.
cycle-detected.suggestion: Break the cycle by replacing an edge with a message-queue (async pattern) or by re-sequencing calls.

single-lb-spof.title: Single load-balancer
single-lb-spof.message: A single load-balancer fronts multiple backends — it becomes a SPOF.
single-lb-spof.description: If this LB fails, all backend traffic becomes unreachable.
single-lb-spof.suggestion: Add a second load-balancer (active/passive or multi-AZ) for high availability.

single-db-spof.title: Single database
single-db-spof.message: A single database serves multiple upstream services.
single-db-spof.description: The DB becomes a single point of failure for the entire stack.
single-db-spof.suggestion: Add a read replica or deploy a multi-AZ cluster.

mq-no-consumer.title: Queue without consumer
mq-no-consumer.message: A message-queue receives messages but has no consumer.
mq-no-consumer.description: Messages will pile up indefinitely — likely a design oversight.
mq-no-consumer.suggestion: Add a background-job or consumer service wired to the queue's output.

container-missing-parent.title: Container missing host-server parent
container-missing-parent.message: A container must be nested inside a host-server.
container-missing-parent.description: Without a host-server parent, the simulation cannot share CPU/memory resources correctly.
container-missing-parent.suggestion: Drag the container inside a host-server.

db-exposed-publicly.title: Database exposed publicly
db-exposed-publicly.message: A database is directly accessible from a client.
db-exposed-publicly.description: Major security flaw: an application service must always sit between clients and storage.
db-exposed-publicly.suggestion: Insert an http-server or api-service between the client and the database.

cache-exposed-publicly.title: Cache exposed publicly
cache-exposed-publicly.message: A cache is directly accessible from a client.
cache-exposed-publicly.description: An exposed cache allows arbitrary read/write of shared data.
cache-exposed-publicly.suggestion: Insert an application service between the client and the cache.

cross-zone-edge-no-gateway.title: Edge crossing public → private zone without gateway
cross-zone-edge-no-gateway.message: An edge crosses the public zone into a private zone without a filter.
cross-zone-edge-no-gateway.description: Public traffic should never reach backend components directly without passing through a firewall, WAF, API gateway, or load-balancer.
cross-zone-edge-no-gateway.suggestion: Insert an api-gateway, firewall, or load-balancer at the zone boundary.

public-service-no-auth.title: Public service without identity-provider
public-service-no-auth.message: A service in a public/DMZ zone is reachable but no identity-provider exists in the architecture.
public-service-no-auth.description: Without an IdP, no standardized authentication mechanism protects this service.
public-service-no-auth.suggestion: Add an identity-provider (OIDC, OAuth2) and wire it to exposed services.

no-cdn-multi-clients.title: Missing CDN for multi-client traffic
no-cdn-multi-clients.message: Multiple client-groups hit the same http-server with no CDN in front.
no-cdn-multi-clients.description: An edge CDN would serve static resources closer to clients and offload the origin server.
no-cdn-multi-clients.suggestion: Add a CDN between clients and the origin server.

db-without-cache.title: Database without upstream cache
db-without-cache.message: A database is hit by a service but no cache exists in the architecture.
db-without-cache.description: Without a cache, every read query hits the DB — avoidable cost and latency.
db-without-cache.suggestion: Add a cache (Redis/Memcached) between the service and the database.

duplicate-node-names.title: Nodes with duplicate labels
duplicate-node-names.message: Two or more nodes share the same label.
duplicate-node-names.description: Duplicate labels make diagrams ambiguous and complicate log/trace correlation.
duplicate-node-names.suggestion: Rename each node with a unique label.

edge-no-protocol.title: Edge without protocol set
edge-no-protocol.message: An application edge has no protocol defined.
edge-no-protocol.description: Without a protocol, the engine cannot apply protocol-specific latencies/costs (REST vs gRPC vs WebSocket).
edge-no-protocol.suggestion: Select the edge and choose a protocol in the properties panel.

duplicate-edge.title: Duplicate edges
duplicate-edge.message: Multiple edges share the same source, target, and protocol.
duplicate-edge.description: Duplicate edges are likely an editing artifact.

metadata.sectionDescription: Cross-cutting annotations: notes, tags, owner, last review. Do not affect simulation.
metadata.tagsHelp: Use commas to separate multiple tags.

list.empty: No decisions yet — create one to document an architectural choice.

related.title: Related ADRs
related.empty: No ADRs linked to this element.

exportMarkdown.tooltip: Export as Markdown (Mermaid diagram + components + ADRs)
exportMarkdown.description: Generates a complete .md document: diagram, components, connections, and ADRs.
